trade.summary: The Stati Uniti is among Spagna's most important trading partners outside the EU. Spagna exports vehicles, food products, pharmaceuticals, and machinery; the Stati Uniti exports aircraft, technology, pharmaceuticals, and industrial machinery. Spagna has a modest trade surplus with the Stati Uniti that has occasionally attracted Stati Uniti attention under protectionist administrations.
disputes: Stati Uniti tariff threats on EU automotive and food exports (2025)

diplomatic.status: Close NATO allies with some value tensions
diplomatic.summary: Spagna and the Stati Uniti are NATO allies with a generally positive relationship. The Stati Uniti maintains key military installations in Spagna (Rota naval base, Moron de la Frontera air base). Under Trump 2.0, Spagna-Stati Uniti ties face pressure over NATO defense spending demands, trade tariffs, and differences on Ucraina policy. Spagna's left-wing government has been somewhat more critical of Stati Uniti positions than some other EU allies.
recent_incidents: Stati Uniti pressure on NATO defense spending targets (2025)

military.summary: The Stati Uniti maintains Rota naval base (key NATO asset with Stati Uniti destroyers deployed for Ballistic Missile Defense) and Moron de la Frontera air base in Spagna. Spagna is part of the Stati Uniti-led NATO command structure and contributes to NATO missions. Spagna cooperates on Ucraina military support within NATO. Stati Uniti-Spagna military cooperation is deep and institutionalized.

financial.summary: Significant bilateral investment. Stati Uniti firms (Google, Microsoft, Amazon) have major Spagna presences including data centers. Spanish firms (Santander, BBVA, Iberdrola, ACS, Ferrovial) have significant Stati Uniti operations — Ferrovial manages Chicago's O'Hare airport, Iberdrola is a major Stati Uniti renewable energy player. Financial ties are deep and growing.

energy.summary: The Stati Uniti has become a significant LNG supplier to Spagna since 2022 as Spagna diversified from Russian energy. Stati Uniti LNG exports to Spagna have grown substantially. Spagna is developing green hydrogen exports where it could compete with or complement Stati Uniti hydrogen ambitions.

scientific.summary: Very strong scientific partnership. Spanish and Stati Uniti researchers collaborate extensively across all disciplines. Many Spanish researchers work at Stati Uniti universities; American Fulbright scholars study in Spagna. Joint research in astrophysics (Canary Islands observatories with Stati Uniti partnerships), climate science, and biomedical research is particularly strong.
